channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Inside the military career of Shamsud-Din Jabbar, from Afghanistan deployment to Staff Sergeant
item.description: Shamsud-Din Bahar Jabbar, a former Army Staff Sergeant, had a distinguished military career from 2007 to 2020, serving as an IT and Human Resources Specialist with deployments in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, Jason Nguyen, KPRC 2

item.title: All Democrats expected to participate in Speaker’s vote
item.description: All Democrats are expected to be in the Capitol on Friday for the vote to seat the next Speaker of the House, according to the Democratic whip’s office.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: FBI found 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home during search in December, prosecutors say
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they have ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month, according to a court filing by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump blames ‘open borders’ for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump blamed the situation at the U.S.-Mexico border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Vegas Cybertruck explosion driver ID'd as Army soldier from Colorado: Source
item.description: Livelsberger allegedly served at one point at the same military base as the man suspected of driving into a crowd in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Soldier in Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion had gunshot wound to head: sheriff
item.description: The man who drove a Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside of a Las Vegas, Nevada hotel has been identified as Matthew Livelsberger, an Army soldier from Colorado, a law enforcement source told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans attack victims: What we know so far
item.description: Investigations continue into an "act of terrorism" in New Orleans' bustling Canal and Bourbon streets after a truck drove into a crowd, killing 15 people and injuring at least 35 more.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: NYPD presence increased at Trump Tower, Times Square after attacks in New Orleans, Las Vegas
item.description: “While there are no immediate threats to our city at this time, out of an abundance of caution, we have heightened security and have increased NYPD presence at relevant locations"
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Cruise ship stomach bug illnesses hit 12-year high: CDC
item.description: Cruise ships saw the highest number of stomach bug illnesses since 2012 with 16 outbreaks impacting a total of 1,894 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Vegas Cybertruck explosion driver ID'd as Army soldier from Colorado: Source
item.description: Livelsberger allegedly served at one point at the same military base as the man suspected of driving into a crowd in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, NewsNation

item.title: Sugar Bowl security to equal Super Bowl: New Orleans police
item.description: The New Orleans Police Department revealed Thursday that the annual Sugar Bowl will feature heightened security after a man drove directly into a crowd on Bourbon Street earlier this week, leaving 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Meet the newest breed in American Kennel Club's lineup
item.description: Say hello to the latest dog in the American Kennel Club's lineup of recognized breeds. Or you might say “hej.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump Hotel guests complain of chaos following Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Guests staying at the Trump International Hotel were forced to evacuate Wednesday morning after a Tesla Cybertruck exploded at the main entrance, killing one person and injuring seven others.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, NewsNation

item.title: Meet the Danish-Swedish farmdog, the newest breed in the American Kennel Club's lineup
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Say hello to the latest dog in the American Kennel Club's lineup of recognized breeds. Or you might say “hej.” The Danish-Swedish farmdog — yep, that's the official name — joined the pack Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Borrowers cheer Biden's record-setting student loan forgiveness, but left bitter with the system
item.description: While grateful to see their loan balance disappear, borrowers who received forgiveness under Biden lament a system they say is holding back millions of others.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: What we know about the victims in New Orleans truck attack
item.description: Authorities are yet to release the names of the 15 victims from the New Year’s Day truck attack on Bourbon Street, but families have started sharing tributes.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, Gage Goulding, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to hold DC rally day before inauguration
item.description: The president-elect has not held a rally in the District of Columbia since he delivered a speech on the Ellipse on Jan. 6, 2021, which preceded a violent attack on the Capitol by supporters attempting to stop the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: John Ramsey reveals why JonBenet murder may be connected to dance classmate's 1997 assault
item.description: JonBenet Ramsey's father, John Ramsey, believes his daughter's 1996 murder case may have possible ties to a separate 1997 assault case that occurred not far from their home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Explosion outside Trump’s Las Vegas hotel investigated as possible terrorism
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Firework mortars and camp fuel canisters were found stuffed into the back of the Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel early Wednesday, killing a suspect inside the vehicle and sparking an intense investigation into possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: No. 6 Ohio State routs top-ranked Oregon 41-21 in the Rose Bowl, advances to CFP semifinals
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Second chances don’t come around often in life, Ohio State coach Ryan Day told his players in a team meeting before the Rose Bowl.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pistons guard Jaden Ivey sustains leg injury in victory over the Magic
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Detroit guard Jaden Ivey sustained what appeared to be a severe injury to his lower left leg in the fourth quarter of Detroit's victory over the Orlando Magic on Wednesday night. Orlando's Cole Anthony slipped and fell as he and Ivey went for the ball.
item.dc:creator: DAVE HOGG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl CFP quarterfinal between Georgia and Notre Dame postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame was postponed a day because of an attack about a mile away from the Superdome early Wednesday, when authorities say a truck driver deliberately plowed into a New Year’s crowd and killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about victims of New Orleans terrorist attack
item.description: Family and friends have begun identifying the victims after a terrorist suspect plowed a rented truck into a crowd of jubilant New Year's Day celebrators in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni set to rest Saquon Barkley in finale and end chance at NFL rushing record
item.description: Saquon Barkley had a heart-to-heart talk with his dad that was more coated in heartbreak than hope over the Philadelphia Eagles running back's pursuit of the NFL single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: An aspiring nurse, football star, single mother and father of 2 killed in New Orleans attack
item.description: An 18-year-old girl dreaming of becoming a nurse, a single mother, a father of two and a former Princeton football star suffered fatal injuries when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street, packed with holiday revelers early Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Death toll in New Orleans attack rises to 15, coroner says; more details emerge as investigation continues
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A driver behind the wheel of a pickup truck rammed into a crowd of New Orleans revelers on Bourbon Street early on New Year’s Day, killing at least 15 people in what the FBI is investigating as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: Law enforcement arrested Forrest Kendall Pemberton after he allegedly traveled to Plantation, Fla., in search of AIPAC’s office with several firearms, including an AR-15 rifle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4

item.title: New Orleans New Year's Day ramming is the latest attack to use a vehicle as a deadly weapon
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Police say a man who drove a pickup truck at high speed into a crowd of people celebrating the new year in New Orleans early Wednesday was hell-bent on creating carnage.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: An aspiring nurse, a football star, a single mother and a father of 2 killed in New Orleans attack
item.description: An 18-year-old woman dreaming of becoming a nurse, a single mother, a father of two and a football star suffered fatal injuries when the driver of a white pickup truck sped onto Bourbon Street, packed with holiday revelers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An armed man kills at least 10 people, including 2 children, in a shooting rampage in Montenegro
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — At least 10 people, including two children, were killed and four others were seriously wounded on Wednesday in a shooting rampage that followed a bar brawl in a western Montenegrin city, officials said. The shooter was on the run.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jocelyne Wildenstein, Swiss socialite known for extreme plastic surgeries, dies at 84: reports
item.description: Wildenstein's facial surgeries were notably feline, leading the press to (cruelly) dub her "Catwoman.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: LeBron James' youngest son Bryce commits to Arizona
item.description: Bryce James, son of NBA star LeBron James, is heading to Arizona. Bryce James announced his decision Wednesday on Instagram and his father had a quick response.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Movies in 2024: Lessons from a turbulent year at the box office
item.description: Movie ticket sales took a bit of a hit in 2024. The annual domestic box office is expected to end up at around $8.75 billion, down more than 3% from 2023, according to estimates from Comscore.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans terror attack in photos
item.description: A driver wrought carnage on New Orleans' famed French Quarter early on New Year's Day, killing at least 10 people as he steered around a police blockade and rammed a pickup truck into a crowd before being shot to death by police, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Watch a replay of the 2025 Rose Parade presented by Honda
item.description: LA's Very Own KTLA 5 is proud to offer this replay of the 136th Rose Parade presented by Honda from New Year’s Day, Jan. 1, 2025. The theme of the 136th Rose Parade was “Best Day Ever!” Billie Jean King, a sports icon and equality champion, was the parade's Grand Marshal.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: Harry Chandler, Navy medic who survived Japan's attack on Pearl Harbor, dies at 103
item.description: Harry Chandler, a Navy medic who helped pull injured sailors from the oily waters of Pearl Harbor after the 1941 Japanese attack on the naval base, has died. He was 103.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you should probably throw away your Christmas tree this week
item.description: With the holiday season now behind us, it might be time to consider losing your Christmas trees, too.
item.dc:creator: Tylor Sorensen, WGN 9
